<findings>

[observation] [warning] Low cohesion (0.27) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
[observation] [warning] Cohesion 0.27 is below the healthy threshold — this zone is an import-graph residual grouping unrelated files (build scripts, a hench store module, viewer UI components) that do not share a domain purpose.
[observation] [warning] Coupling 0.73 exceeds the high-coupling threshold — the cross-package mixing of hench and web files artificially inflates the boundary surface; resolving the misclassifications should bring coupling down significantly.
[observation] [warning] packages/hench/src/store/suggestions.ts is grouped with web package files despite belonging to the hench store layer — this misclassification should be corrected so the hench agent zone fully captures its own store.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "web-build-infrastructure" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [warning] Dissolving this zone is a prerequisite for any coupling trend analysis to be meaningful across the web package — the 0.73 coupling score conflates two independent architectural signals (hench-store misclassification and viewer-component placement) into one metric that cannot be acted on without disambiguation.
[suggestion] [info] packages/web/src/landing/index.html should be explicitly reclassified to the web-viewer zone (as the app shell that bootstraps viewer components) when the phantom zone is dissolved, not left as an unclaimed file. Its current placement in build-infrastructure incorrectly implies it is a build artifact rather than a runtime entry point.
[suggestion] [critical] Zone "Web Build Infrastructure" (web-build-infrastructure) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.73, cohesion: 0.27, coupling: 0.73) — requires immediate architectural intervention
[relationship] [warning] The co-location of packages/hench/src/store/suggestions.ts with web package files in this zone creates a false import-graph edge between the hench and web tiers. Any zone-coupling metric that includes this zone will over-report cross-tier coupling until suggestions.ts is reassigned to hench-agent.
[anti-pattern] [warning] The phantom zone's 0.73 coupling score is being counted in aggregate web-package health metrics. Any CI gate or automated report that sums or averages zone coupling will treat the web package as unhealthy due solely to a community-detection artifact, not a real architectural problem. This false signal may cause teams to ignore legitimate future coupling regressions because the baseline is already flagged.

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.27) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- Cohesion 0.27 and coupling 0.73 are both outside healthy bounds — this zone is a community-detection residual that mixes web build scripts, a hench store file, and viewer components that the developer hints confirm belong in web-viewer
- packages/hench/src/store/suggestions.ts and its test are misclassified here; they belong in the hench-agent zone alongside the rest of the hench store layer
- The viewer files listed as entry points (elapsed-time.ts, route-state.ts, tick-timer.ts, task-audit.ts) should migrate to the web-viewer zone per developer hints — their presence here inflates coupling and suppresses the true cohesion of both zones
- Cohesion 0.27 is below the healthy threshold — this zone is an import-graph residual grouping unrelated files (build scripts, a hench store module, viewer UI components) that do not share a domain purpose.
- Coupling 0.73 exceeds the high-coupling threshold — the cross-package mixing of hench and web files artificially inflates the boundary surface; resolving the misclassifications should bring coupling down significantly.
- packages/hench/src/store/suggestions.ts is grouped with web package files despite belonging to the hench store layer — this misclassification should be corrected so the hench agent zone fully captures its own store.
- Zone "web-build-infrastructure" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- The zone acts as a phantom bridge in the import graph: because suggestions.ts (hench) and web viewer files share this zone, graph-traversal algorithms can infer a hench→web dependency path that does not exist at runtime. This is a tooling artifact — the actual architectural boundary between hench and web is clean — but it makes the boundary statically invisible and untestable until the misclassification is resolved.
- The co-location of packages/hench/src/store/suggestions.ts with web package files in this zone creates a false import-graph edge between the hench and web tiers. Any zone-coupling metric that includes this zone will over-report cross-tier coupling until suggestions.ts is reassigned to hench-agent.
- Because coupling is measured zone-to-zone, the 0.73 coupling score on this phantom zone propagates into any aggregate health report for the web package — tools that sum or average zone coupling will incorrectly classify the entire web package as architecturally unhealthy. The misclassification is not just a display issue; it degrades the signal quality of any automated health gate that relies on coupling thresholds.
- The phantom zone's 0.73 coupling score is being counted in aggregate web-package health metrics. Any CI gate or automated report that sums or averages zone coupling will treat the web package as unhealthy due solely to a community-detection artifact, not a real architectural problem. This false signal may cause teams to ignore legitimate future coupling regressions because the baseline is already flagged.
- packages/web/src/landing/index.html is grouped in this phantom zone alongside build scripts — as a static HTML app-shell it is structurally unrelated to build infrastructure and also unrelated to the TypeScript viewer components it bootstraps, making it an orphan in any zone assignment until the phantom zone is dissolved
- The phantom zone's 0.73 coupling score is being attributed to a mix of two unrelated coupling relationships (hench-store→web and viewer-components→web) — any per-zone coupling trend analysis will conflate these two distinct architectural signals into a single noisy metric until the zone is dissolved
- packages/web/src/landing/index.html should be explicitly reclassified to the web-viewer zone (as the app shell that bootstraps viewer components) when the phantom zone is dissolved, not left as an unclaimed file. Its current placement in build-infrastructure incorrectly implies it is a build artifact rather than a runtime entry point.
- Dissolving this zone is a prerequisite for any coupling trend analysis to be meaningful across the web package — the 0.73 coupling score conflates two independent architectural signals (hench-store misclassification and viewer-component placement) into one metric that cannot be acted on without disambiguation.
- [call graph] 62 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)
